I am given 1440 minutes each day, 168 hours each week, 8760 hours each year, and aproximately 25,915 days in my life. Which means I have approximately 3,702 Mondays, 3,702, Tuesdays, 3,702, Wednesdays, 3,702 Thursdays, 3,702 Fridays, 3,702 Saturdays, & 3,702 Sundays! What I choose is what I get. Therefore, I take care of my most valuable resource, not my money, & not even my time, but my energy.

I know I have the ability to achieve the object of my definite purpose in life; therefore I demand of myself persistent, continuous action toward its attainment, and I HERE AND NOW PROMISE TO RENDER SUCH ACTION! I know that if my thoughts control my actions, I am weak, but, if my actions control my thoughts, I am strong. I will greet this day with love in my heart, persist until I succeed, enjoy being God's greatest miracle, live as if today were my last, master my emotions, laugh at the world, multiply my value 100x, pray for guidance, and take action today.  I realize the dominating thoughts of my mind will eventually reproduce themselves in outward physical action, and gradually transform themselves into physical reality; therefore, I will concentrate my thoughts for 20 minutes daily, upon the task of thinking of the person I intend to become, thereby creating in my mind a clear mental picture. I know through the principle of autosuggestion, any desire that I persistently hold in my mind will eventually seek expression through some practical means of attaining the object behind it; therefore, I will devote 10 minutes daily to building self-confidence, self-awareness, & self-realization! I have clearly written down a description of my definite chief aim in life, and I will never stop growing, even after I have developed sufficient self-confidence for its attainment. I fully realize that no wealth or position can long endure, unless built upon truth, justice, & value; therefore, I will engage in no transaction which does not benefit all whom it affects. I will succeed by attracting the forces & energies to myself that I wish to use, and the cooperation of other people. I will induce others to serve me, because of my willingness to serve others. I will give more than I receive, and seek success as bad as I want to breathe. I will eliminate negativity, hatred, envy, jealousy, selfishness, and cynicism, by developing love for all humanity, because I know that a negative attitude toward others can never bring me success. I will cause others to believe in me because I will believe in them, and in myself. I will sign my name to this formula, commit it to memory, and repeat it aloud at least once per day, with full faith that it will gradually influence my thoughts and actions so that I will become a self-reliant, and successful person on my own terms! My Big Picture, Personal Mission Statement, & Personal Vision Timeline

God did not intend for us to fail, or wallow in poverty, self-pity, self martyrdom, or mediocrity in any form. Such is not the grand design for men. He is blessed with all the raw materials necessary for progress, such as imagination, ideas, inspiration, and undeveloped intellectual capacity, and that capacity is totally without limitation. The only limitation placed on our abilities is our inability to easily recognize our unlimited nature. 
It takes effort to become aware of our staggering and limitless abilities. It takes effort to become enthusiastic over a cause or an occupation. It takes effort to continue when our results as well as our friends, tell us to give up trying. It takes effort to feel right about everything that happens, the joy as well as the sorrows of life. And it also takes effort to learn to love ourselves above all others, especially when we are so consciously aware of our failures, doubts and tragedies. It does not however take effort to fail. It requires little else than a slowly deteriorating attitude about our present, our future, and about ourselves.

“I live according to the 7 F’s: Faith, Family, Friends, Fellowship, Fun, Fitness, and Finance” - Randall Pinkett

In the seven cows story in Genesis 41, we are introduced to a strange story of thin and ugly cows that were eating the fat and healthy cows. Although this metaphor provides a bizarre scenario, it provides a very crucial life lesson. While cows don’t eat cows in real life, God had to use this story to reveal a very deep truth. Life is like the story of these seven cows. The thin cows represent those aspects of our lives that are neglected, while the fat cows represent those areas of our lives that we nurture. Just like the sad account of the fat cows being eaten up by the thin ones, those aspects or areas of our lives that we neglect end up eating those that we nurture. The scripture in 1 Corinthians 12:12, tells that although a body is made up of many parts, it is actually one entity. What happens to one part of the body, happens to the whole. If a negative thing affects one part of the body, the whole body gets affected. If a positive thing happens to one part, the whole benefits. Our lives are like that. They are an integrated system. They are made up of many aspects and all these aspects make up one whole. When we concentrate on a few aspects, the ones we neglect will eat those that we concentrate on. The many aspects of our lives can be grouped into seven main areas:  
1. Our Spirituality 2. Our Health 3. Our Finances 4. Our Career 5. Our Family 6. Our Relationship 7. Our Hobbies. 
Real success is having success in all the seven. Many people concentrate on a few at the expense of the others but they end up paying a hefty price because those areas that are neglected start to interfere with those that are nurtured.

They think MONEY is the root of all evil.
They believe POVERTY is the root of all evil.

"The average person has been brainwashed to believe rich people are lucky or dishonest," Siebold writes.
That's why there's a certain shame that comes along with "getting rich" in lower-income communities.
"The world class knows that while having money doesn't guarantee happiness, it does make your life easier and more enjoyable."
They think selfishness is a vice.
They think selfishness is a virtue.

"The rich go out there and try to make themselves happy. They don't try to pretend to save the world," Siebold told Business Insider. The problem is that middle class people see that as a negative--and it's keeping them poor, he writes.
"If you're not taking care of you, you're not in a position to help anyone else. You can't give what you don't have."
They have a LOTTERY mentality.
They have an ACTION mentality.

"While the masses are waiting to pick the right numbers and praying for prosperity, the great ones are solving problems," Siebold writes. "The hero [middle class people] are waiting for maybe God, government, their boss or their spouse. It's the average person's level of thinking that breeds this approach to life and living while the clock keeps ticking away."
They think the road to riches is paved with formal education.
They believe in acquiring specific knowledge.

"Many world-class performers have little formal education, and have amassed their wealth through the acquisition and subsequent sale of specific knowledge," he writes. "Meanwhile, the masses are convinced that master's degrees and doctorates are the way to wealth, mostly because they are trapped in the linear line of thought that holds them back from higher levels of consciousness...The wealthy aren't interested in the means, only the end."
They long for the good old days.
They dream of the future.

"Self-made millionaires get rich because they're willing to bet on themselves and project their dreams, goals and ideas into an unknown future," Siebold writes.
"People who believe their best days are behind them rarely get rich, and often struggle with unhappiness and depression."
They see money through the eyes of emotion.
They think about money logically.

"An ordinarily smart, well-educated and otherwise successful person can be instantly transformed into a fear-based, scarcity driven thinker whose greatest financial aspiration is to retire comfortably," he writes.
"The world class sees money for what it is and what it's not, through the eyes of logic. The great ones know money is a critical tool that presents options and opportunities."
Earns money following directions.
Earns money following their passions.

"To the average person, it looks like the rich are working all the time," Siebold says. "But one of the smartest strategies of the world class is doing what they love and finding a way to get paid for it."
On the other hand, middle class take jobs they don't enjoy "because they need the money and they've been trained in school and conditioned by society to live in a linear thinking world that equates earning money with physical or mental effort."
They set low expectations so they're never disappointed.
They are up for the challenge.

"Psychologists and other mental health experts often advise people to set low expectations for their life to ensure they are not disappointed," Siebold writes.
"No one would ever strike it rich and live their dreams without huge expectations."
They believe you have to DO something to get rich.
They believe you have to BE something to get rich.

"That's why people like Donald Trump go from millionaire to nine billion dollars in debt and come back richer than ever," he writes.
"While the masses are fixated on the doing and the immediate results of their actions, the great ones are learning and growing from every experience, whether it's a success or a failure, knowing their true reward is becoming a human success machine that eventually produces outstanding results."
They believe you need money to make money.
They use other people's money.

Linear thought might tell people to make money in order to earn more, but Siebold says the rich aren't afraid to fund their future from other people's pockets.
"Rich people know not being solvent enough to personally afford something is not relevant. The real question is, 'Is this worth buying, investing in, or pursuing?'" he writes.
They believe the markets are driven by logic and strategy.
They know they're driven by emotion and greed.

Investing successfully in the stock market isn't just about a fancy math formula.
"The rich know that the primary emotions that drive financial markets are fear and greed, and they factor this into all trades and trends they observe," Siebold writes.
"This knowledge of human nature and its overlapping impact on trading give them strategic advantage in building greater wealth through leverage."
They live beyond their means.
They live below their means.

"Here's how to live below your means and tap into the secret wealthy people have used for centuries: Get rich so you can afford to," he writes.
"The rich live below their means, not because they're so savvy, but because they make so much money that they can afford to live like royalty while still having a king's ransom socked away for the future."
They teach their children how to survive.
They teach their kids to thrive.

Rich parents teach their kids from an early age about the world of "haves" and "have-nots," Siebold says. Even he admits many people have argued that he's supporting the idea of elitism.
He disagrees.
"[People] say parents are teaching their kids to look down on the masses because they're poor. This isn't true," he writes. "What they're teaching their kids is to see the world through the eyes of objective reality--the way society really is."
If children understand wealth early on, they'll be more likely to strive for it later in life.
They let money stress them out.
They find peace of mind in wealth.

The reason wealthy people earn more wealth is that they're not afraid to admit that money can solve most problems, Siebold says.
"[The middle class] sees money as a never-ending necessary evil that must be endured as part of life. The world class sees money as the great liberator, and with enough of it, they are able to purchase financial peace of mind."
They would rather be entertained than educated.
They would rather be educated than entertained.

While the rich don't put much stock in furthering wealth through formal education, they appreciate the power of learning long after college is over, Siebold says.
"Walk into a wealthy person's home and one of the first things you'll see is an extensive library of books they've used to educate themselves on how to become more successful," he writes.
"The middle class reads novels, tabloids and entertainment magazines."
They think rich people are snobs.
They just want to surround themselves with like-minded people.

The negative money mentality poisoning the middle class is what keeps the rich hanging out with the rich, he says.
"[Rich people] can't afford the messages of doom and gloom," he writes. "This is often misinterpreted by the masses as snobbery.
Labeling the world class as snobs is another way the middle class finds to feel better bout themselves and their chosen path of mediocrity."
They focus on saving.
They focus on earning.

Siebold theorizes that the wealthy focus on what they'll gain by taking risks, rather than how to save what they have.
"The masses are so focused on clipping coupons and living frugally they miss major opportunities," he writes.
"Even in the midst of a cash flow crisis, the rich reject the nickle and dime thinking of the masses. They are the masters of focusing their mental energy where it belongs: on the big money."
They play it safe with money.
They know when to take risks.

"Leverage is the watchword of the rich," Siebold writes.
"Every investor loses money on occasion, but the world class knows no matter what happens, they will aways be able to earn more."
They love to be comfortable.
They find comfort in uncertainty.

For the most part, it takes guts to take the risks necessary to make it as a millionaire--a challenge most middle class thinkers aren't comfortable living with.
"Physical, psychological, and emotional comfort is the primary goal of the middle class mindset," Siebold writes.
World class thinkers learn early on that becoming a millionaire isn't easy and the need for comfort can be devastating. They learn to be comfortable while operating in a state of ongoing uncertainty."
They never make the connection between money and health.
They know money can save your life.

While the middle class squabbles over the virtues of Obamacare and their company's health plan, the super wealthy are enrolled in a super elite "boutique medical care" association, Siebold says.
"They pay a substantial yearly membership fee that guarantees them 24-hour access to a private physician who only serves a small group of members," he writes.
"Some wealthy neighborhoods have implemented this strategy and even require the physician to live in the neighborhood."
They believe they must choose between a great family and being rich.
They know you can have it all.

The idea the wealth must come at the expense of family time is nothing but a "cop-out", Siebold says.
"The masses have been brainwashed to believe it's an either/or equation," he writes. "The rich know you can have anything you want if you approach the challenge with a mindset rooted in love and abundance."
Hopes Others Fail
Help Others Succeed

They believe watching up to 5 hours of TV per DAY is ok because they "work hard" during the day. They watch others enjoying their lives by living their dreams on the TV to escape the fact that they never reached their own dreams.

They know that watching less than 5 hours of TV per WEEK, and specifically what they watch should be limited and controlled because their mind changes based on what they put into it and therefore generally only watch things to learn.
They read an average of less than 1 book per year. And most of the time it's either assigned reading and/or a novel for entertainment.
They read an average of 1 book per month at a bear minimum, and many read 1 book per week! And they generally read books to learn specific things; from self development, to specific skill sets.

"[The middle class] tells people to be happy with what they have," Steve said. 
"And on the whole, most people are steeped in fear when it comes to money."

Let's get through the class warfare smokescreen. We need to regain our roots and encourage people to invest and build. There is no monopoly on becoming a millionaire. If you're jealous of those with more money, don't just sit there and complain; do something to make more money yourself — spend less time drinking, or smoking and socializing, and more time working. Become one of those people who work hard, invest and build, and at the same time create employment and opportunities for others. - Gina Rinehart
References:

"Rich Dad, Poor Dad" by Robert Kiyosaki 
"Secrets of the Millionaire Mind" by T. Harv Eker
Ways Rich People Think Differently by Business Insider & "How Rich People Think" by Steve Siebold

The 4 Personality Types (S.T.A.R.)
The Four personality types and how to deal with them!
I Marked Each Box that Matches Me with a *
Which TYPE Do I Have the Most Matches?
Structured
They are extremely organized
They like systems
They like to work with people who have won before
These people usually analyze organization over everything!
They feel that is the TOP level!
Most will feel that nobody can do it as good as them!
They feel THEY ARE THE PINNACLE and nobody is as good!
Technical
They are very analytical
They want to know everything about a product / service
They want to know all the details
They want to know AM I competent and do I understand the concepts and/or the real details behind the idea!
Inside and out in an entrepreneur
If the numbers and information isn’t 100% on point I’ll lose them!
Rich on paper cash poor
They GET ANNOYED EASILY!
Action Oriented
Loud, life of the party types
They are networkers
They meet everyone
They are very competitive
They probably love sports
They have a lot of energy and sometimes rub people the wrong way
THEY GET VERY BORED EASILY!
Relationship
They love listening
They love hugging
They love kisses
They love gifts
THEY ARE GREAT WITH PEOPLE!
They are great for business development and building relationships!
They get offended very easily!
The 4 Different Personality Type Quadrant!
Relationship Oriented
Yellow = Relationship Oriented + Indirect & Less Confrontational, Slow Talker, & Slow Moving.
Blue = Relationship Oriented + Direct, More Confrontational, Fast Talker, & Fast Moving.

*Please note, nobody fits perfectly into these categories BUT I will find that not only do I fit one most of the time but so does everyone else. Therefore, I can see who others are based on a personality demographic and appeal to them more. Which builds rapport & real relationships, while also keeping me ahead of most people who aren't paying attention to these factors!
Green = Task / Business Oriented + Indirect & Less Confrontational, Slow Talker, & Slow Moving.
Red = Task / Business Oriented + Direct, More Confrontational, Fast Talker, & Fast Moving.

*I first analyzed myself and paid attention to who I am. After I figured out where I fit best in this quadrant I made a note here: "I, Jared Sanborn, am a RED, therefore, I now know how I best fit in with other people. To take advantage of this fact, I will also pay attention to others and thus, mold myself to all situations and be all to all as a very wise man named PAUL once said. This will enable me to maximize every relationship I am in for myself, but more importantly, for them."

I have always been an entrepreneur by nature as I always wanted to be my own boss. I did it the wrong way and spent 4 years in prison because of those bad decisions BUT I’ve been free for many years now and have totally turned my life around. Not many ever overcome the penal system. Not many ever get past that lifestyle and not many ever realize they have like 75% of the skillset needed to succeed in real business. Such as, risk-taking, keeping one's own schedule and making decisions to better a business’s growth, judgment of character (they aren’t using it right, but they have it), they also can’t be fooled easily. Therefore, many of the original skills needed but misapplied. Thank God for helping me overcome this.

I opened my own business pure bread entertainment

Fear Setting - A Lesson in Stoicism

The hard choices -- what I most fear doing, asking, saying -- are very often exactly what I need to do. 
How can I overcome self-paralysis and take action? 
I fully envision and write down my fears in detail, in a simple but powerful exercise called "fear-setting." 
I use this practice to help myself thrive in high-stress environments and separate what I can control from what I cannot.

Rather than get up and give any type of recipe for success or highlight reel, this is my recipe for avoiding self-destruction, and certainly self-paralysis.
I've found the most reliable safety net for emotional free fall is actually the same tool that has helped me to make my best business decisions. 
But that is secondary. And it is ... stoicism.   
We suffer more often in imagination than in reality - Senaca the Younger

I visualize the things I fear the most and take them to their worst case scenario to ensure they no longer paralyze me from taking action!
3 exercises to understand that some of your fears are real but MOST are not what you think they are!
Easy Choices = Hard Life... Hard Choices = Easy Life!
Exercise 1: What if I...
Exercise 2: The Benefits
Exercise 3: The Cost of Inaction

List the benefits of an attempt or partial success
The atrocious cost of not changing anything

Step 2: Prevention
Step 3: Repair

I created 4 eulogies that i would like to be read at my funeral from 4 different people: 1 family, 1 friend, 1 business partner, 1 client
Family
Friend

A (husband, father, brother, nephew, cousin, etc) who always took the time to ensure that if I truly needed help and was willing to put in the effort he would match my effort and point me in the right direction or to the person who could help me. A man who knew the value of family and built all of his companies as legacies for us. To immortalize the Sanborn name... but more importantly to immortalize himself on our hearts as a caring, loving, understanding person who always wanted to see everyone around succeed to even greater lengths than he did. A son who scared the daylights out of us many times yet never waivered in the face of risk or danger. A father who changed his entire life for his daughter Lily. A husband who stuck by his wife through the best and the worst and supported his family not only financially, but emotionally, mentally and spiritually. A family member who wished the best for all but understood he couldnt DO the best for all. A brother who, once he got it all together, was there for me and wanted to see me reach my own potential. An uncle who helped Tristan realize his true potential and gave him permission to soar! A business man who was ruthless only in the sense of making sure his companies were bettering mankind. A cousin who always had the craziest ideas but the work ethic to match and was always fun to play cards with. A man who is defined more by the wisdom he has attained than the money he has made. A man who's character is bigger than his bank account.

My friend Jared cared more about my success than his. He continually sacrificed his own agenda to ensure all our agenda's and goals were accomplished. He didnt waste time but if you put forth effort and needed help, he was the first to lend a helping hand. My friend Jared cared more about humanity than he did his businesses and used his businesses to improve humanity. Jared was family and treated me like a (brother, sister, mother, father), his deep care for the needs of others is what made him someone i am not only honored to say i knew as a friend but to call my brother. Someone who wasn't afraid to tell me the truth in love, and care enough about me to point out the cliff in front of my path, but did it in a kind enough way to remind us all that he walked off a few cliffs in his time as well. A friend who truly embodied friendship in a way that is lost a lot in todays society. IE. the friend that flatters you or just yes's you for the sake of not creating ripples... He wasn't that person. He would absolutely call you out on your nonsense and would accept no excuses since he believed in extreme ownership and responsibility to direct your own life. I remember one of his favorite sayings was: You are the sum total of all the thoughts you’ve ever had in life and you are where you are RIGHT NOW because that’s where you want to be, whether you want to admit it or not. With philosophies like this he HAD to make everyone around him better by default as he would continually push us all to our next level

Task
Points
I must be at least a month ahead
Step 1
Rate the task in terms of importance
Points
Critical
Necessary
Important
Helpful
Marginal
Step 2
Decide the tasks urgency regarding when it must be done
This month
Next month
This quarter
Next quarter
End of year
Step 3
Multiply the rate of importance times the rate of urgency
IE. 5 (critical) x 4 (next month) = 20
Step 4
Decision Scale
A - critical task to be finished by end of month
B - important task to be finished by end of quarter
C - low priority to be finished by end of year
D - DROP IT... not worth doing

complete this sentence: Our brand is the ONLY _________
that ____________. 
In the first blank, put the name of your category (frozen pizza, furniture dealership, computer repair service). In the second blank, describe your zag (that tastes like Naples; that sells sustainably manufactured furniture: that makes house calls). If you can't keep it brief and use the word ONLY, then you don't have a zag. Your best option in that case is to make a list of all the competitors who could make the same claim, then start to shift your strategy away from theirs. —- WHAT is your category? HOW are you different? WHO are your customers? WHERE are they located? WHEN do they need you? and wHY are you important?

FOR HARLEY-DAVIDSON
WHAT: The ONLY motorcycle manufacturer
HOW: that makes big, loud motorcycles
WHO: for macho guys (and macho "wannabees")
WHERE: mostly in the United States
WHY: who want to join a gang of cowboys

### Quick Win 5: Make 5 High-Quality Comments Today
Don't wait until you have a post ready. Go comment on posts by these 5 accounts right now:
1. **Pascal Bornet** (linkedin.com/in/pascalbornet) - #1 LinkedIn Top Voice in AI & Automation
2. **Andrew Ng** (linkedin.com/in/andrewyng) - LinkedIn Top Voice 2025, DeepLearning.AI founder
3. **Ethan Mollick** (Wharton AI researcher) - 500K+ followers, academic credibility
4. **Ajit Kumar** - 345K+ followers, known for simple practical AI advice
5. **Reid Hoffman** - LinkedIn founder, AI investor, huge reach in exact Jared's audience
**Comment formula**: Hook (acknowledge their specific point) + Add (one insight from your experience) + Ask (one genuine question). Minimum 3 sentences. Never generic.
**Example comment for Pascal Bornet's automation post**:
This resonates with what I'm seeing in practice. When I delegated our entire
client communication workflow to my AI partner last quarter, the surprise wasn't
the efficiency gain - it was realizing I'd been spending 40% of my CEO time on
coordination that wasn't strategic. The interesting follow-up question: do you
think the biggest ROI from AI automation is time saved, or decision quality
improved?
### Quick Win 6: Post the "Connection Activation" Post
This is a one-time post you should deploy within the next 3 days to re-engage dormant connections who've never seen your content.
**Template** (ready to use, adjust numbers/details):
I've been building something unusual for the past [X] months.
Most founders talk about using AI. I've been running my entire business
alongside one.
Not as a tool. As a partner.
This week I'm posting specifically about what that actually looks like:
the wins, the surprises, and the things that didn't work.
If you're a founder, executive, or business leader trying to figure
out what AI partnership actually means in practice - stick around.
That's exactly what I'll be covering.
What's the one thing you're most curious about when it comes to
running a business with AI?
(Genuinely asking - your answer shapes what I cover next.)
This post does three things: activates algorithm, builds audience expectation, and generates comment-based research into what your audience actually wants to read.
### Quick Win 7: Set Up 3 Saved Searches for Engagement Triggers
LinkedIn's search can be saved to alert you when people post about specific topics. Set these up:
1. "AI implementation" - engage as an expert
2. "AI tools for business" - engage as a practitioner
3. "AI strategy" - engage as a thought leader
Spend 20 minutes/day engaging with fresh posts on these topics. You'll surface in relevant feeds, build relationships with active posters, and be seen as the person who actually knows this stuff.

Real Relationships! - How I feel is influenced by my associations.

The people with whom I choose to associate are a major source of what I know and how I feel. 
Therefore, if I can't CHANGE my Friends, CHANGE my Friends!

Remember the "Gap Fact"! - People who Remain the Same, Stay Friends | People who Both Grow, Stay Friends | BUT, if one remains the same and the other grows, there is no common ground for friendship and thus over time a gap occurs in which the friendship will dissipate. OR you can make the controlled decision to part ways!
This practice is good to do once per week with one person in each category! Personal & Professional!
Three major questions which those in search of a better life must constantly ask about their relationships with regards to others:
Who can I personally help reach their goals? (list one person)
How can I personally help them reach their goals? (describe an action to take)
Who can I connect them with to help achieve their goals? (list their match)

Who am I around? (list one person)

What effect are they having? (describe how they effect you or could)

Better than many of the wrong friends are a few of the right friends. Better than a few of the wrong friends are no friends at all. 
- Jim Rohn

Unpopular as it may seem, each of us accumulated people, customs, attitudes, habits, opinions, and philosophies which we very simply cannot afford to keep, if we seek honestly after an improved life. Friendships are indeed valuable, but so is the human life and it is foolish to fall short of our potential because we feared alienating a good friend. 
- Jim Rohn

In countless ways, we wander through life allowing people and their attitudes and ideas to mold our character, people whose attitudes and ideas have brought themselves little in the way of progress, productivity, or happiness. 
- Jim Rohn

Those with poisonous attitudes, strange opinions, and caustic conversations love to look for someone nice who will listen to them. They love to dump their verbal garbage into the mental factory of anyone willing to listen. 
A major challenge in life is for each person to learn the art of standing guard at the doorway of their mind. Carefully examine the credentials and authority of those seeking to enter within that place where your attitudes are formed. 
- Jim Rohn

How can I become an excellent communicator / orator?
Watch Videos about Communication
Communicate with excellent communicators
Study those same individuals
Learn about logic, rational thought processes, reasoning
Study logical fallacies and learn to avoid committing most of them UNLESS I AM using them for a reason
Read books about communication
Study presenters
Study salesmen - I am authentic with individuals which is the number one thing I can be to learn about what someone wants / their needs and IF i’m the right guy to fulfill those needs!
Practice communication with my wife! - FIND OUT what she needs and provide that!
Watch videos on Delegation
Practice slowing down the verbal communication of my super fast thoughts!
Prepare for what I AM communicating! OR when, if a speaking engagement!
Learn to tell a story! - Beginning, middle and end!
Listen to excellent communicators
Spend more time around excellent communicators
Listen to my daughter and try to decipher / help her with her inquiries! - Her communication isn’t great yet, but my perceiving of communication skills can be honed!
Read books, blogs, articles, etc in general! - Start reading MORE!
Organize my thoughts / list of things to do, that way I can easily convey / communicate my intentions to ANYONE ELSE!
LISTEN TWICE as much as I TALK! - This way, I am clear on what the person is saying or meaning before I RESPOND!

Be Honest about everything possible! - Honesty / transparency will convey a trust in the other person which should allow for easier communication between the other party and myself which in turn refers me back to Listen twice as much as I talk!

How can I forgive others and know I’ve truly forgiven them?
Stop bringing up the past to anyone, especially my wife!

Don’t dwell on what someone “owes” me.
Money or time or anything.
Consider all past “debts” / slights against me learning experiences.

Consider the fact that CHRIST truly forgives me!
If Christ can forgive me, who am I not to forgive someone else!

Let the person who did something against ME know personally, I have forgiven them, and that I will no longer focus on whatever it is that needed forgiveness.
This will add an accountability on my own word TO that person that I won’t bring it up again!
To do so again, makes me a hypocrite and a liar!

Confess to GOD sincerely that I am forgiving others and letting go of the past hurts from EVERYONE!
Let this be the accountability to CHRIST my savior that I DID THIS!

Tell other people / someone else I have forgiven ANOTHER person. 
This adds a third layer of accountability to ACTUALLY forgive and let bygones be bygones!

When I am mad, DO NOT bring up the past. 
Calm down and focus on what we can do to fix the situation at hand!

Pay attention to the things I AM saying! If I DO start to bring up the past and/or slights against ME, STOP! 
Pause and gather myself to realize IF I continue I will be a hypocritical liar who BLEW my credibility and accountability to GOD, that person, and others.

Write down what someone did to ME, then write down, I FORGIVE that person for what it is they did to me. 
IE. My wife has lied to me many times, got drunk in and out of the house many times, and cheated on me, BUT I FORGIVE HER! 
This is now on record by my own words and my own statement that I HAVE FORGIVEN HER!

Stop trying to connect the PAST as REASONS for why I DO THINGS today!
While this is logical to do so in many respects, the more I DO THIS, the more I give power to my past decisions and actions and/or OTHERS past decisions and actions against me! 
REDEFINE how I think regarding TODAY! And let today NOT BE AFFECTED by yesterday, no matter what it was!

Simply Focus on the Present and the tasks at hand!
By doing so, I WILL have NO TIME to think about ANYTHING from the past, let alone the bad things!

Remember that to truly embrace what is the present and prepare for the future I must let go of the past! 
The action step is actually embracing today and preparing for the future so as to leave no time for the past!

FORGIVE MYSELF! How? 
Understand that what has become of your brother, mother, father, or anyone else from your past, no matter your actions in their lives had choices just like you. 
They could continue to dwell on past problems and hold onto grudges or let go and grab hold of their future!
Forgive others for doing things in their life that made me believe it was MY FAULT! 
It never was, nor can it ever be my fault. 
I may have had a bad influence at one point or another, BUT, that influence was momentary and their state of mind COULD also be temporary IF THEY EVER CHOSE TO CHANGE!

Continue to show empathy in understanding I was once where they are and forgive that they haven’t moved on yet. Forgive that sadly, not everyone will grow and/or as fast as others. Even though it took me many years as well.

Pay attention to the things I say, are they present and future focused or past focused? 
If focused on the past, change it, continue to focus on the present and the future. 
Just like writing my goals in the present, positive and personal tense changes the meaning subliminally for my brain, so does the direction of my focus. 
I AM A PRESENT ENJOYING, FUTURE FOCUSED INDIVIDUAL!

Ensure to bring up less people in sentences that have the word “BECAUSE” in it! 
This doesn’t mean I can never say “Because Dan Martell / Brian Tracey / My Wife said, or because i read this somewhere by such and such author etc..” But rather STOP using phrases like “Because my father doesn’t know how to communicate I never learned”. Instead say, “I KNOW HOW TO COMMUNICATE, in spite of any past challenges.”
The keys being how it’s rephrased and leaving out any names and/or direct references to someone who I previously would have blamed for any perceived shortcomings.

REMOVE the phrases “I Can’t”, “It’s Impossible”, “I Don’t Know How” BECAUSE.. From my vocabulary!
IE. If the words I Can’t Because or It’s Impossible Because or I Don’t Know How Because are predicated on the assumption that due to my past experiences WITH OTHER PEOPLE somehow it’s true, it shows I HAVEN’T FORGIVEN either those people or myself for those situations!
Remove this language from your vocabulary and replace it with things like: 
Even though it appears difficult, either I will find the solution via JUST IN TIME learning OR I will ask someone who already has accomplished this before OR I will hire someone who can provide the solution!

I KNOW I’ve forgiven myself and others when I don’t feel that helping someone else is like trying to PAY them back for any past harm I caused to them and/or others.
When I embrace the fact that CHRIST paid the price to forgive all sins, including mine against others, I have no need to pay back my debts at they were already paid. 
This also doesn’t mean not to do good / help others, but it means that when doing it, do it with JOY and feeling as a blessing that God can use me help someone else!

I KNOW I’ve forgiven others when I accept ALL RESPONSIBILITY FOR ALL MY ACTIONS at any point in my life! 
Blaming anyone for anything at any time shows a lack of responsibility, accountability and also concedes that they have more control over my life than I DO! 
Only Christ has more control over me than I Do and I will not give that right to anyone else who doesn’t deserve that honor! Christ is KING.. who are they?

To determine your objective ask yourself the following questions
Why am I going there?
What do I want to achieve?
Why do I want to have that conversation?
Why do I want to write that letter?
Why do I want to meet with this person?
Why do I want that interview?
Why do I want to address this meeting?

If two or more of your answers to these questions are the same, you have found your objective. There can only be one objective and it must be clear cut and specific. When you have found it, give it one final check. The operative word for that check is almost always why. Once your objective is clear, once you know why, you can begin to prepare your message. Balance everything you plan to say against your objective.  If your thoughts and words do not introduce, reinforce, or help you achieve your objective, go back to the drawing board. And when you know what your objective really is stick to it.

Remember that when your problems are in your head, they never seem to solve themselves and they only tend to cause you to worry and they can even make you sick. But once you list them and write them down you have changed your worries from internal problems, into another form. once your problems are in written form you can look at them and see what they are from a much better perspective and position to overcome them because your brain doesn't have to focus on WHAT they ARE, but rather HOW to FIX THEM! You will feel a great sense of relief once your problems are written down. The tension dissappears and a great weight will be lifted from your shoulders.

Seven Levels Deep Thinking Process - Digging Deep
This strategy is to help bring clarity regarding goals, overcoming obstacles, or simply drilling to the core of any matter.
This helps get right to the root of the cause and change things from the root. Afterall, it makes no sense to pluck the weeds if I leave the roots.

I wrote down my goal / obstacle / challenge 
For Example: "Write a New York Times Bestseller", or "Stop Smoking" etc. Then ask the question WHY?

I then wrote an answer to my first "Why?". 
After that, I ask "Why?" again, regarding my answer. 
(I then repeated this for a total of 7 levels/times.)

Another Way to Phrase this is 
"Why is this important to me?"
Question and/or Statement/Goal:
Why? - Level  1
Why? - Level  2
Why? - Level  3
Why? - Level  4
Why? - Level  5
Why? - Level  6
Why? - Level  7
I wrote a New York Times Best Seller

I want to write a book that inspires people to live their lives judiciously. To spend their time wisely with the understanding that we’re all dying just a little bit each day. I want the world to know that it does no-one any good to live small. Or live in the outfit of someone who they’re not.

It’s important for me to share this message, because it’s been the biggest message in my own personal life. Life is short. We don’t know for sure what comes next. But we do know that we have the now. The present. To be whoever, however we want to be.

It’s the most liberating feeling in the world to be yourself. To say no to the voice inside that makes you want to play small. I’ve lived most of my life believing that I need to heed others’ direction. That their experience is more important than my own. I want to save people from a lifetime of that heartache, because I believe that’s the most tragic human experience, to live not knowing your own value and worth.

My dad lived a really hard life to make ours a little bit easier. It’s important for me to make his life count. I need to prove that his experience was real. His experience was what made me who I am today. What he sacrificed for me was real. To make him happy as long as he’s here. As long as I’m here.
I need to prove that life is fair. That God takes care of everyone.
I need to know that I’m not in a rat race. I have to know that what I do matters. That there’s meaning in my life, and in everyone else’s life.
Because it proves that I exist. That this isn’t just fabrication. I matter.
Risk Management Tool
I thought about how to minimize risk without minimizing risk taking!
I make up my mind as follows:
I ask the following questions to ensure my risk taking aligns with rational thought processes, my goals, my purpose, and my why!
I answer the questions on the left to ensure my risk taking aligns with rational thought processes, my goals, my purpose, and my why!
What’s the best thing that can happen as a result of taking this risk?
What’s the most likely thing to happen as a result of taking this risk?
What’s the worst thing that can happen as a result of taking this risk?
I make up my mind as follows:
If the most likely thing to happen will get me closer to my goals and if I’m willing to live with the worst thing that can happen, I march onwards.
If the most likely thing to happen will not get me closer to my goals, it’s futile for me to be discussing this risk. I move on.
If I am not willing to deal with the worst thing that can happen, I must run away as fast as I can.
Success is Secular. Significance is Spiritual.
A Strategic Plan for Success
Success doesn’t come to you; you must go to it. The trail is well traveled and well-marked. If you want to walk it, you can. - Nido Qubein
When implementing my strategic plan for success, it really comes down to three D’s:
Success focuses on three F’s: fans, fame, and fortune.
1. Decide what I want most to achieve!
Success is focused on tasks, even goals
2. Determine the first step to getting what I want.
Significance also focuses on three F's: faith, family, and friends.
3. Do the first thing that will start me moving toward my goal.
But significance focuses on purpose.
To choose success and significance I must be a strategic thinker who:
Significance forces me to ask the following questions:
1. Has a clear vision of what I want to accomplish!
Why am I here?
2. Develops a solid strategy that answers three questions:
What do I do with the talents, experiences and skills that I have?
Who or what am I today?
How can I make the world a better place?
Who do I want to become?
How do I plant seeds of greatness in the lives of those around me?
How do I get there?
How do I make an impact in the circles of influence where I find or place myself?
3. Employs practical assistance to achieve my goals.
How do I give to the world something that will last longer than myself?
4. Commits to consistent execution because in consistency, success emerges.

Do I Think Progressively Toward Myself?
Can I honestly say I am a more valuable person today than 3 or 6 months ago?

I have become someone others can look to for answers on some subjects and I am becoming more and more logical / rational and less emotionally driven every day. YET I have my emotions.

I have laid it out in this Learning to Live Life planner and it revolves around at least 25 minutes a day of reading books, a note taking plan for the books I read, a review process for certain books that have challenges in them, watching and taking notes on seminars, and listening to audio books, seminars, and speeches at least 2 hours a week. In addition, I watch videos from my mentors and share them in my #Grind group which lets me see more of that information again.

I have goals from 1 year to 10 years.

I am only a member of the companies I work with so on that note, yes. others I will have to answer in the future.

My annual self-analysis is an essential in the effective marketing of personal services, as is annual inventory in merchandising. Moreover, my yearly analysis should disclose a decrease of faults, and increase in virtues. I will either go ahead, stand still or goes backward in life. My object should be, of course to go ahead. My annual self-analysis will disclose whether advancement has been made and, if so, how much. It will also disclose any backward steps I may have made. The effective marketing of personal services requires me to move forward even if the progress is slow. My annual self analysis should be made a the end of each year so I can include any real New Year’s Resolutions and improvements that the analysis indicates should be made. If I have answered all these questions truthfully, I know more about myself than the majority of people. I studied the questions carefully and came back to them once each year, to be astounded at the amount of additional knowledge of great value to myself I will have gained by the simple method of answering the questions truthfully. If I am not certain concerning the answers to some of the questions, I will seek the counsel of those who know me well, especially those who have no motive in flattering me, and see myself through their eyes. The experience will be astonishing.

I have ABSOLUTE CONTROL over but one thing, and that is my thoughts. This is the most significant and inspiring of all facts known to man! It reflects man's Divine nature. This Divine prerogative is the sole means by which I may control my own destiny. If I fail to control my own mind, I may be sure I will control nothing else.

If I must be careless with my possessions, let it be in connection with material things. My mind is my spiritual estate! Protect and use it with the care to which Divine Royalty is entitled.

I was given a WILL-POWER for this purpose.

Remember, it is one thing to want money – everyone wants more – but it is something entirely different to be WORTH more. 
Many people mistake their wants for their just dues. 
Your financial requirements and wants have nothing whatsoever to do with your worth. 
Your value is established entirely by your ability to render useful service, or your capacity to induce others to render such service. 
- Napoleon Hill, Think & Grow Rich, 1937.
The OfFISHial Go Fish Philosophy
This entire page is inspired by "Fish Tales" by Stephen Lundin
A way to make work fun! At the job, or at home!
The Questions
The Answers
Are there things at work or home you take for granted but without these things your life wout have less abundance?
Are thre times you are doing one thing and already thinking about he next, thereby losing all that he present moment has to offer?
Are there people who serve you every day whom you don't really see and without whom much of what you take for granted would come to a halt?
The Fish Inventory and Goals Exercise: Part One
Rate on a Scale of 1-5

Play: 
1 = This place is so uptight that play is a four letter word
5 = The atmosphere here is lighthearted and playful. It brings a smile to my face just thinking about it.

Make Someone Else's Day: 
1 = Customers and colleagues (or friends and family) are treated with indfference or as an interruption
5 = Customers and colleagues (or friends and family) are treated in a way that leaves them feeling special.

Be There: 
1 = People here seem so distracted it is hard to know if they are listening. 
5 = You are the sole focus of attention when talking with someone here.

Choose Your Attitude: 
1 = Workers (or friends and family) demonstrate the mental maturity of a two year old having a bad day. 
5 = There is a high level of accountability and everyone knows she or he chooses her or his attitude.
The Fish Inventory and Goals Exercise: Part Two
Statements / Goals

Pick the principle whose rating you would like to improve the most and write a statement about it. 
For example: you may write: "I chose the 2 that I gave to "make someone's elses day" because I think there is a lot of room for improvement.

Now, set some goals and make some commitments about what you will do this week to move that category in a positive direction. These should be things you can do without anyone elses's help. List a couple of goals to start.
Example: I will choose two coworkers / friends and do something special for them.
Find Ways to Play at Work and at Home
25 Goals Work Set
25 Goals Home Set
Simply list as many ways you can think of to play either at work or at home to make your life more fun!
Write 25 in each of the next two columns for a total of 50 goals to be more fun.
Have Some Fun
5 Goals Work Set
5 Goals Home Set
Take five of the ideas you listed last week and implement one each day from Monday-Friday at work and at home.
Record the weekly highlights in the boxes to the right
At the end of the week tell a colleague and/or friend/family member about your experiences.
Intend to Make Someone's day
Work & Home Names
Ideas
When you have INTENTION, opportunities show up!
Think of the people in your life whose day you would like to make.
List their names to the right and when an idea emerges write it down.
Thenm when the time is right, go for it.
Random Acts of Kindness
Work Kindness
Home Kindness
This week is dedicated to random acts of kindness at a place they are greatly needed: work and the home.
At the end of the week you are allowed to write down your favorite randme act of kindness and tell a friend about it.
This can be a random act of kindness you performed, or was performed TO you.
Why Can't We Just Be Where We Are?
Ideas
This week is dedicated to being where you are. Thnk of all the people with home you interact each week at work or home.

When someone comes into your office or room to talk, either say "this is not a good time" OR shut down your computer and ignore your phone while conversing with them. If you need to take a call, explain that ahead of time. Move around your desk into a good "be there" position.
Consider all the work settigns you visit. This activity is designed to make all of those interactions more effective, less anxious, and more pleasant.
Always disclose the amount of time you have for a conversation and ask if it is enough.
In the next column there are some ideas that you may either try or use to stimulate your own.
When making quick exchanges in the hall or between rooms, position your body so you can fully see the other person.
After each episode, as the person for whom you are being there, if they would mind reflecting on the experience.
During conversations, clear your mind of everything but the topic at hand, then do it again and then again.
This reflective feedback will help you catch any little distractions and sharpen your ability to be where you are.
Never take a cell phone to lunch unless you really need to. But always keep it off
If you are in an open area, try not to look past the person with whom you are speaking.
Use the person's name as often as you can without getting weird.
How Fascinating! Be There Now!
Ideas
There is little tension or anxiety in the place we call the present. Stay away from worrying about the future and come back to the present.
Say "How fascinating" and return to the present! Stop putting off things with your children for other things
Study "The Power of now" by Eckhart Tolle and write some insights about it.
Do you have a full deck?
The Attitude I Have
The Attitude I Choose
Prepare five cards in your attitude deck. Three by five cards are perfect for this task.
Out of all the possible choices, pick five attitudes you would like to see more often in your life.
After preparing the cards simply look at them throughout the day at some set intervals. Perhaps every hour or so.
Each time ask yourself " what attitude do I have right now and would I be better off with one of the five in my hand?"
If you don't like the one you have, choose another, but first write down the name of the one you have as best you can.
It's Not About Choosing a Positive Attitude
Put a white board up somewhere others can see and regularly post your chosen attitude for all to see.
See if your example leads others to start posting theirs below yours!
Who are you being while you are doing whatever you are doing?
The Role I am Playing
My New World Record
Why not set a world record?!
This is the week to improve upon your own world records.
Improve your world record of positive contribution to the department or your household. etc
Ask yoruself "Who would I be if I was being world record me?"
Do this for all your major roles and keep a record of highlights for use during your next performance review.
Tag, you're it! Light some Fish! Fires
What Am I Wearing?
Why am I wearing it?
Find something to wear that reminds you of your commitment to living your life fully and that causes others to comment and ask you "what is that about?"

Each time someone asks you why you are wearing an octopus on your head, or whatever, you have an opportunity to recommit to your personal vision and at the same time start a small fire in the heart and spirit of another.
Over time you will find your commitment deepening.
When others ask why you are so upbeat, tellt hem about the choices you are making.
It may help them see their own choices.
And when you are blessed with an opportunity to help another human being to see her or his potential, take a minute to do that. It may be the most powerful legacy you leave on earth.
It is my hope for you that you catch your limit of life, every day!
Life is too precious to just be passing through!

What we have become as a result of all of our past experiences and how we have handled them. For things to change, we must change, that is one of life’s fundamentals. - Jim Rohn

IT'S TIME FOR ME TO TAKE MASSIVE ACTION!
Quotes about MASSIVE ACTION and/or directed & focused action!

Never has there been a map, however carefully executed to detail and scale, which carried it owner over even 1 inch of ground.
Never has there been a parchment of law, however fair, which prevented one crime. 
Never has there been a scroll, even such as the ones I read in the Og Mandino Challenge, which earned so much as a penny or produced a single word of acclamation. 
Action and action alone is the tinder which ignites the map, the parchment, the scroll, my dreams, my plans, my goals, into a living force. 
Action is the food and drink which will nourish my success. I must act now. 
- Og Mandino

Not what he wishes and prays for does a man get, but what he justly earns. 
His wishes and prayers are only gratified and answered when they harmonize with his thoughts and actions. 
- James Allen

A good idea not acted upon produces terrible psychological pain. 
But a good idea acted upon brings enormous mental satisfaction 
- David Schwartz

Average people have a lottery mentality. Rich People have an action mentality. 
- Unknown

When it’s all said and done, will you have said more than you’ve done, or done more than you said? 
- Unknown (maybe Jim Rohn)

If knowledge made you successful, then librarians would be some of the most successful people alive.
These books can help you learn calculated action but they can’t take or do anything for you! 
It's all about Action my friend! 
- Unknown

Every time we choose action over ease or labor over rest, we develop an increasing level of self-worth, self-respect and self confidence. 
Any day we choose we can develop a new discipline of doing rather than neglecting. 
In the final analysis, it is how we feel about ourselves that provides the greatest reward from any activity. 
It is not what we get that makes us valuable, it is what we become in the process of doing that brings value into our lives.
It is activity that converts human dreams into human reality and that conversion from idea to actuality gives us a personal value that can come from no other source. 
- Jim Rohn

Affirmation without action is the beginning of DELUSION! 
Statement without action is the beginning of hypocrisy and cognitive dissonance!
Which is the beginning of further self doubt because your sub conscious doesn’t even believe you anymore! 
YOU literally DOUBT you because you never follow through on what you say or think! 
- Jim Rohn

Think like a person of action, act like a person who thinks! 
- Jim Rohn

Future intention determines present action. Resolve to do, what you resolve to do!
- Brian Tracy

The most important part of learning is WHAT you do after learning whatever you learned! 
ALL successful people are INTENSELY ACTION ORIENTED.
They move fast on anything to capitalize on the energy from good ideas etc 
Taking action can only lead to success or failure. 
You can not lose from taking action. Either it works and you do more of it, or you fail, learn quick, get smarter and try something new. 
People either take action on IDEAS immediately or NEVER! 
- Brian Tracy

"You must teach people by the school of example, for they will learn at no other!" 
- Albert Schweitzer

Action cures fear! Indecision, postponement,on the other hand, fertilizes fear. 
- David Schwartz

Good luck can be enticed by excepting opportunity. Men of action are favored by good luck. 
- George S. Clason

See our walk talks and our talk talks but our walk talks louder than our talk talks! 
- Donna Krech

Life is like a field of newly fallen snow… Where I choose to walk every step will show! 
- Jim Rohn

Take action! Ideas are worthless unless we act on them. 
- Earl Nightingale

When you start things, see them through!  
GO THE DISTANCE on the things you start! 
If you don’t see it through, it’s like building a foundation and then walking off and leaving it! 
Imagine, building a foundation with no walls, no roof, no doors etc. Literally foundations scattered all over the country! 
I guess you could say you specialize in starting but never finishing! That is a skill which will bring you NOTHING! 
- Jim Rohn (paraphrased)

In the end we are all the sum total of our actions. 
Character cannot be counterfeited, nor can it be put on and cast off as if it were a garment to meet the whim of the moment. 
Like the markings on wood which are ingrained in the very heart of the tree, character requires time and nurturing for growth and development. 
- Madame Chiang Kai-Shek

Man is shackled only by himself: 
thought and action are the jailers of fate, they imprison, being base; 
they are also the angels of freedom, they liberate being noble. 
- James Allen

Weak is he who permits his thoughts to control his actions; strong is he who forces his actions to control his thoughts. 
- Og Mandino

Words are no substitute for action, and ACTION is no substitute for words! 
- Jim Rohn

As soon as I change my actions, it changes the world around me. 
  Therefore, take in better information, create new ideas, and then expand on growth.
Manage the INPUT to manage the OUTPUT!
Put new ideas into my mind from other people but eliminate the fear mongering and garbage thoughts.
Small incremental actions every day make HUGE changes in the long run! 
- Jim Rohn

Remember action feeds and strengthens confidence; inaction in all forms feeds fear. 
Therefore to fight fear, act. To increase fear wait, put off, postpone, procrastinate. 
- David Schwartz

Nobody will ever be complimented or praised for not disturbing anyone or not taking action or waiting to be told what to do. 
- David Schwartz

Always try to make the world better by your actions. 
- Earl Nightingale

A man is not doing much until the cause he works for possesses all there is of him. 
- John Wanamaker

Nothing comes merely by thinking about it.
- John Wanamaker

Only poverty and idleness embitter life. 
- John Wanamaker

Do not wait; the time will never be just right. 
Start where you stand, and work with whatever tools you may have at your command, and better tools will be found as you go along. 
- Napoleon Hill

Procrastination is just a word for taking the time to talk yourself out of something! 
- Patrick bet David

What’s the use of a plan if you do not work it? 
- John Wanamaker

It is good to have a systematic way of doing things, but it is more important to be sure that you do them. 
- John Wanamaker

Learn to tell the difference between activity and work. 
- John Wanamaker

A man who believes he can standstill on any height he has risen to, doesn’t understand the situation. 
- John Wanamaker

You can't build a reputation on what you are going to do. 
- Henry Ford

"An idea not coupled with action will never get any bigger than the brain cell it occupied.” 
- Arnold H. Glasgow

Procrastination is the art of living in your yesterdays, avoiding you’re today’s, and ruining your tomorrow’s. 
- Unknown

Procrastination is a time stealer, and it simply means to put off intentionally doing something that should be done. 
- Unknown

The measure of who we are is what we do with what we have. 
- Vince Lombardi

"It is the greatest of all mistakes to do nothing because you can do only a little. Do what you can." 
- Sydney Smith

“Tomorrow is the day when idlers work, and fools, reform, and mortal men lay hold on heaven.” 
- Edward Young

“He who begun has half done. Dare to be wise; begin.” 
- Horace

“Anything worth putting off is worth abandoning altogether.” 
- Epictetus(AD 55 – AD 135)

“Delay not till tomorrow to be wise; tomorrow’s sun to thee may neve rise.” 
- William Congreve (1670-1729; Letter to Cobham)

“He who puts off nothing till tomorrow has done a great deal.” 
- Baltasar Graci (1601-56)

“You can’t get much done in life if you only work on the days when you feel good.” 
- Jerry West, NBA Star

“Procrastination is something best put off until tomorrow.” 
- Gerald Vaughan

“If you have goals and procrastination you have nothing. If you have goals and you take action, you will have anything you want.” 
- Thomas J. Vilord

“Procrastination is the fear of success. 
People procrastinate because they are afraid of the success that they know will result if they move ahead now. 
Because success is heavy, carries a responsibility with it, it is much easier to procrastinate and live on the ‘someday I’ll’ philosophy.” 
- Denis Waitley

Be unique based on your actions! 
Let people know you’re set apart not with your words but with your actions! 
If you add more value than anyone else you’re unique! 
- Unknown

Action is the Antidote to Despair! 
- Joan Baez
Some of my personal Quotes about action and work ethic!

Get off of SOMEDAY Isl (Island/I'll) and make today the day you do what you say! 
The only people on Someday Isl are complainers, time wasters, explainers, and excuse makers! 
You must leave Someday Isl or you'll never make it to Today Isl (I'll / Island). 
Afterall, you can't be in two places at once!
On Today Isl people do more than they talk! 
Therefore, there is no time for complaining, time wasting, explaining, and excuse making, only doing, creating, and problem solving! 
- Jared Sanborn

Your desire to make a change must be greater than your desire to remain the same! 
Thus change is literally a decision with actions that follow the decision! 
- Jared Sanborn

Be a firefly and shine bright while you fly. 
Because when sitting still, you’re light is blind to the eye.
Remember you only give off light while in flight. 
So use your wings, and try to reach new heights. 
Therefore, fly and shed some light on things,
Because sight, and new life, is what your actions bring! 
- Jared Sanborn

Never give someone else the satisfaction of their actions determining your actions! Your reactions should be based on WHO you are, NOT what they did! 
- Jared Sanborn

Work ethic is essentially equal among the general populace and the successful. 
The difference is in WHAT they work on. 
The general populace will work hard on his/her job, 8 hours a day, 5 days a week, and therefore, their job is the only thing they can become great at. 
The successful will work hard on his/her SELF, all day, every day, and therefore they can become great at anything they desire. 
Thus, the acquiring and mastering of new skills enables upward motion towards and the actual living of their own definition of success. 
- Jared Sanborn

Change is easy as the decisions you make but as hard as following through on those decisions. 
The follow-through proves IF you ever truly made the decision to change or not!
If you never follow through, you’ve proven that while you may have thought about change and/or even stated you will change, YOU NEVER ACTUALLY DECIDED TO CHANGE! 
If you did follow through, you’ve proven you actually made a decision and your character forced you to stand by that decision and bring actual measurable and desired change. 
After all, the actions prove who you really are, not what you say, but what you do! 
Decisions require action, OR they were never a decision! Decide today to change! Then go do it! 
- Jared Sanborn

People can’t teach you what they don’t know themselves. 
So do not blame your parents or others for not teaching you things you wished you learned earlier! 
Instead go out and find someone who posesses the skills you want to learn and learn from them. 
And you may ask “well if I was never taught them, how will I know what they look like.” 
Trust me you’ll know. 
For example, if you wanted to find a great father to learn how to be a great father. 
How could you find out for sure they are a great father? 
Ask the children and wife how they feel about them! 
Or just listen to them for a bit and see how different they are from the example you had regarding fatherhood! 
And once you find them, learn! 
Instead of blaming people who couldn’t teach you even if they wanted to (most probably wished they could have), 
start learning from those who can teach you! 
Take action to change instead of placing blame! 
- Jared Sanborn

If you work hard on your job, you can make a living! 
If you work hard on yourself, you can make a killing! 
- Jared Sanborn

It's best to do more than you think you know, than to think you know more than you do! 
- Jared Sanborn

Treat people more like brothers and sisters. Pay value to your spouse or a loved one today with a touch and a simple "I love you".
Kiss a child good night with an added you’re so special and I love you for who you are.
Tell a parent or relative in person or by phone how much he or she means to you today.
Spend time listening to and giving encouragement to an elder each week.
Make a contribution to something or someone for which there is no direct pay off or obligation.
This Saturday do something you have wanted to do for years and repeat this process once each month.

Experiment with five or six hours sleep per night while being kind your body with good nutrition and exercise. Over a lifetime the additional productive hours available amount to 12 more years of life.
Either learn an additional language and study the customs of countries you plan to visit, or continue to improve your mastery over your dominant language.
There are those who will laugh at those who read useful books, and yet there is little difference between those who cannot read and those who will not read it, the result of both is ignorance.
There are those who will discourage those in search of a better occupation, and yet it is essential that each of us find what we were meant to do if true happiness is to be found.
There are those who are frowned upon those who set ambitious goals, and yet without goals there can be no achievement, and without achievement life will be as it has been.
There are those who will gossip about those who are doing well, and yet there can be no cause for rejoicing among those who are doing poorly.
There are those who will cry to those who turn away in search of a better life, and yet we must sometimes turn away from those whose effect limits us, in spite of the tears.
There are those who hate those who achieve the improved life, and yet there can be little happiness in poverty, nor love among those who must endure it.

There is a little difference between one who has given up his life and one who has given up his hope. 
- Jim Rohn

The greatest value of the past is how wisely we invest it in the future. Let the past be a servant for making the future both more enjoyable and profitable. 
- Jim Rohn
